entry.title: ROSEBURG HAD WARM WEEKEND, LIGHT SHOWERS ARE AHEAD
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 4:00 a.m. Roseburg had warm weather over the weekend, but cooler temperatures are ahead for the next couple of days. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the mercury reached 70 degrees on Saturday and 66 degrees on Sunday at the Roseburg...

entry.title: COUNCIL TO CONSIDER TAX-EXEMPTION FOR APARTMENT COMPLEX
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Roseburg City Council will consider a tax exemption for the Sunshine Park Apartments as part of its meeting Monday night at 7:00 p.m. In 2019, Wishcamper Development Partners contacted the city regarding a low-income housing project being planned in partnership with the Opportunity Council, a 501c3 nonprofit corporation. It...

entry.title: CITY OF ROSEBURG AND TENNIS CENTER UNVEIL NEW OUTDOOR COURTS
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 3:30 a.m. The City of Roseburg and the Umpqua Valley Tennis Center unveiled the new $2.53 million outdoor tennis and pickleball courts with a ribbon-cutting ceremony last Thursday. A city release said the project to renovate the public courts more than half a century old was made possible when the city teamed...

entry.title: ROSEBURG ARBOR DAY PHOTO CONTEST BEGINS THURSDAY
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 3:20 a.m. The Roseburg Parks and Recreation Department has the 2026 Arbor Day Photo contest beginning on Thursday, March 12th. A city release said Roseburg has an amazing variety of lovely trees – including those found in city parks. Photo enthusiasts are invited to capture a shot of their favorite tree and...

entry.title: BLM FEBRUARY 2026 TIMBER SALES BRING IN OVER $8.3 MILLION
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 3:05 a.m. The Bureau of Land Management sold 27.6 million board feet of timber across 1,255 public acres in Oregon in February, for a total of over $8.3 million in revenue. A BLM release said this indicates a strong demand for American lumber manufacturing by exceeding total appraised values by over $3...

entry.title: MERCY FOUNDATION ANNOUNCES FILM PREMIERE FOR STUDENT CREATOR PROJECT
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 2:50 a.m. CHI Mercy Health Mercy Foundation has announced the film premiere for the Student Creator Project Public Service Announcements on Wednesday, March 11th: A Mercy release said this unique project is a collaborative effort between the foundation, Spiderking Studios and Douglas Public Health Network. It has provided youth with free, hands-on...

entry.title: CHILDREN TO RECEIVE HANDMADE “READING BUDDY WHALES”
entry.summary: March 8, 2026 4:30 p.m. This week children across Douglas County will experience a special celebration of reading thanks to a county-wide Whale Tour led by longtime SMART Reading volunteers and coordinators Nadine and Duke DeVaney. A SMART release said the couple will travel throughout the county delivering handmade crocheted whale “reading buddies” to children...

entry.title: SCOTTSBURG MAN ESCAPES AFTER COURT APPEARANCE, CAPTURED FOLLOWING CHASE IN DOWNTOWN ROSEBURG FRIDAY
entry.summary: March 8, 2026 4:15 p.m. A Scottsburg man escaped after a court appearance on Friday and was captured after a chase in the downtown Roseburg area. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said just after 9:30 a.m. 44-year-old Matthew Bigelow left the Circuit Court room in the courthouse on Southeast Douglas Avenue and had been...

entry.title: SCOTTSBURG MAN ESCAPES AFTER COURT APPEARANCE, CAPTURED FOLLOWING CHASE IN DOWNTOWN ROSEBURG
entry.summary: March 9, 2026 4:15 p.m. A Scottsburg man escaped after a court appearance on Friday and was captured after a chase in the downtown Roseburg area. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said just after 9:30 a.m. 44-year-old Matthew Bigelow left the Circuit Court room in the courthouse on Southeast Douglas Avenue and had been...

entry.title: DRAIN MAN DIED FRIDAY IN VEHICLE CRASH NEAR YONCALLA
entry.summary: March 8, 2026 3:45 p.m. A Drain man died in a single-vehicle crash near Yoncalla on Friday afternoon. Undersheriff Brad O’Dell of the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office said shortly before 2:45 p.m. dispatchers received a report of the wreck in the 10000 block of Elkhead Road. O’Dell said the crash had been discovered by a...

entry.title: Sheriff's office investigates fatal crash near Yoncalla
entry.summary: YONCALLA — On Sunday, the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office (DCSO) announced in a press release that a Drain man was killed Friday in a single vehicle crash near Yoncalla.

entry.title: Roseburg's new $2.53M outdoor courts facility opens
entry.summary: Roseburg’s new $2.53 million outdoor tennis and pickleball courts are now open, quickly becoming a hub for community sports and inclusion, with strong support from local leaders and Special Olympics participants.

entry.title: Umpqua Woofstock Expands Mission With New Spring Fair in Sutherlin
entry.summary: Umpqua Woofstock will host its first Spring Fair April 24-26 at Oak Hills Golf Club in Sutherlin. The new event reflects the nonprofit’s continued growth from a local pet festival into a year round effort supporting pets and families across the Umpqua Valley.

entry.title: Original Winston Summer Nite Cruise Route Restored For 2026 Event
entry.summary: The original cruise route is back for the 2026 Winston Summer Nite Cruise. The restored layout returns cruisers to Abraham and Douglas Boulevard past longtime Winston landmarks. The event is July 11, with registration at Grocery Outlet.

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.06.62
entry.summary: Live from Washington D.C., Second District Congressman Cliff Bentz talks about the situation in Iran, snowpack issues in Oregon, his O&C legislation, and more.

entry.title: Douglas High School 2025-26 first semester honor roll
entry.summary: Douglas High School announced the honor roll for the 2025-26 first semester. These students qualified:

entry.title: Katy's Cheesecake Company expands from home baking to brick-and-mortar Roseburg shop
VEHICLE LEAVES HIGHWAY IN THE SCOTTSBURG AREA
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:50 a.m. No one was hurt when a vehicle left the highway in the Scottsburg area on Thursday. An Oregon State Police report said just after 1:30 p.m. a sedan was westbound on Highway 38 near milepost 15. The driver claimed that a pickup had entered the westbound lane while going eastbound....

entry.title: SUTHERLIN POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:40 a.m. Sutherlin Police jailed a man for a warrant on Thursday. An RPD report said just before 10:00 p.m. officers stopped a vehicle for a traffic violation at the intersection of South Calapooia Street and West Everett Avenue. The driver was found to be a 44-year-old who had a valid felony...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR DRIVING WHILE SUSPENDED
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for driving while suspended on Thursday. An RPD report said just before 1:00 p.m. officers stopped a vehicle near the intersection of Northwest Estelle Street and Northwest Black Avenue, after recognizing that the driver’s license had been revoked. The status was confirmed through records, and...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOLLOWING ALLEGED THEFT INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man following an alleged theft incident on Thursday. An RPD report said at 1:10 p.m. officers contacted the 50-year-old after he was captured on video surveillance stealing a bottle of liquor from a store in the 1300 block of Northeast Stephens Street. He was taken into...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN AFTER DISTURBANCE ON THURSDAY MORNING
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:10 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man after a disturbance on Thursday morning. An RPD report said at 11:40 a.m. officers responded to the incident in the 2500 block of Northeast Stephens Street. 22-year-old Johnny Cornell left the area prior to officer’s arriving. Dispatch advised that Cornell had two warrants for his...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR REPORTED DISTURBANCE
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 10:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man following a reported disturbance on Thursday afternoon. An RPD report said at 2:50 p.m. officers responded to the Roseburg Regional Airport in the 2100 block of Northwest Aviation Drive. Two men verbally arguing. At one point, 42-year Thomas Landrum allegedly pulled out brass knuckles,...

entry.title: MYRTLE CREEK MAN JAILED FOLLOWING REPORTED DISTURBANCE
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 9:40 a.m. A Myrtle Creek man was jailed following a reported disturbance on Thursday night. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said just after 8:30 p.m. 41-year-old John Ireland and a victim got into an argument in the 900 block of Bilger Creek Road in the Myrtle Creek area. During the argument,...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOLLOWING ALLEGED STRANGULATION INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 9:15 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man following an alleged strangulation incident on Thursday night. An RPD report said at 9:20 p.m. the 56-year-old allegedly held a victim down by her throat following an argument. The suspect then reportedly grabbed a pillow and placed it over the victim’s face and made additional...

entry.title: LOCAL REAL ESTATE TODAY 3.06.62
entry.summary: Mary Gilbert of the Mary Gilbert Group at Keller Williams Southern Oregon has our weekly look at local real estate:

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.06.26
entry.summary: Dr. Jason Heald talks about the Umpqua Singer’s Concert at UCC Friday night, and shares information about their upcoming trip to Ireland.

entry.title: Fiction for junior readers
entry.summary: Roseburg Public Library has more than 6,000 fiction titles on the shelves for junior readers, from early chapter books to big, world-building novels.

entry.title: Police Log: March 5, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported 11 incidents in the Mar. 5 log, including menacing, strangulation, theft, warrants, prohibited camping, driving while suspended, and a disturbance at the Roseburg Airport involving brass knuckles, according to the City of Roseburg.

entry.title: Student Creator Project to premiere teen-made PSAs at Roseburg Cinema
entry.summary: The creativity and voices of local youth will take to the big screen March 11 as the Student Creator Project premieres a series of original public service announcements created by Douglas County teens.

entry.title: Roseburg Arbor Day Photo Contest Opens March 12
entry.summary: Roseburg Parks and Recreation will open its 2026 Arbor Day Photo Contest on March 12, inviting people to submit one photo of a tree or grove of trees taken in the Roseburg area. Entries are due by 5 p.m. April 17, with winners to be announced on Arbor Day, April 24.

entry.title: Douglas Forest Protective Association Opens Hiring For 2026 Fire Season
entry.summary: DFPA is hiring for the 2026 fire season, with seasonal openings for wildland firefighters, dispatchers and wildfire detection camera operators. Applications are open now, with reviews starting in April, interviews in May and onboarding in June.

entry.title: From hobby to career; inside Atlantis Pet Store
entry.summary: First time customers of Atlantis Pet Shop may walk in expecting to find a brand new cat or dog. Instead, they’re met with a jungle.

entry.title: SMART Reading volunteer prepares for county-wide Whale Tour
entry.summary: Nadine DeVaney worked hard for months to create a new experience for the students they help.

entry.title: March 6, 2025
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: UMPQUA SINGERS PRESENT: “FROM AMBER WAVES TO THE EMERALD ISLE”
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 6:00 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: The Umpqua Community College Performing and Visual Arts present the Umpqua Singers in concert on Friday night at 7:00 p.m. in Centerstage at the Whipple Fine Arts Center on the UCC campus. The Umpqua Singers, directed by Dr. Jason Heald, will present “From Amber Waves...

entry.title: LAUKA CONVICTED OF MURDERING TERRY LEA DEANE IN 2023
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 3:50 a.m. The News Review is reporting: The verdict is in: Dwayne Michael Lauka is guilty of murdering Terry Lea Deane in her Oakland home in August 2023. Thursday’s verdict followed more than two weeks of testimony and involved more than two dozen witnesses, all but a handful called by prosecutors. Deane’s...

entry.title: COW CREEK TRIBE WORKING ON FORMER RITE AID BUILDING
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of Indians are doing rehabilitation work on the former Rite Aid building on Southeast Jackson Street in downtown Roseburg. Cow Creek Communications and Marketing Director Lindsay Campman told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that crews are currently working on asbestos removal...

entry.title: COMMISSIONERS HOST 5TH ANNUAL MUSIC AT THE COURTHOUSE
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 3:30 a.m. On Wednesday, Douglas County Commissioners hosted their 5th annual Music at the Courthouse concert series at the Douglas County Courthouse. A county release said the event featured nine local music programs in celebration of National Music in our Schools Month. The commissioners issued a proclamation prior to the first performance...

entry.title: DFPA ACCEPTING APPLICATIONS FOR 2026 FIRE SEASON
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 3:20 a.m. The Douglas Forest Protective Association is now accepting applications for the 2026 fire season. A DFPA release said the agency has been dedicated to protecting lives, resources, and property from wildland fires through practical prevention and aggressive fire suppression since 1912. DFPA has several seasonal roles available: *Wildland firefighter *Wildland...

entry.title: H.O.P.E. DRUG COURT PROJECTS CONCLUDE THIS WEEK
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 3:05 a.m. The 112th class of the H.O.P.E. Drug Court program is wrapping up its community service projects this week, marking the culmination of hard work, recovery, and community engagement. Nathan Sessions will host a community spaghetti feed fundraiser Friday from 5:00 p.m. to 7:00 p.m. at Liberty Christian Fellowship, at 831...

entry.title: GREATEST OF THE GRAPE HAPPENING SATURDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 2:50 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: The Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association has its 55th annual Greatest of the Grape on Saturday at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville and tickets are still available. Oregon’s oldest yearly celebration of wine will take place from 6:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. Nineteen wineries are...

entry.title: Work resumes on Oregon 42 Lookingglass Creek to I-5 Project
entry.summary: GREEN DISTRICT — A contractor working for the Oregon Department of Transportation (ODOT) has resumed work on the Oregon 42 Lookingglass Creek to I-5 Project.

entry.title: Roseburg's 2026 Arbor Day Photo Contest begins March 12
entry.summary: Roseburg Parks and Recreation has put out a call for treescape photos for its 2026 Arbor Day Photo Contest.

entry.title: Umpqua Symphony Association presents Fandango! on March 14
entry.summary: WINCHESTER — The Umpqua Symphony Association will present Fandango! in concert at 3 p.m. Saturday, March 14 at Centerstage Theater on the Umpqua Community College campus.

entry.title: Roseburg Fire Department hydrant flushing to begin April 1
entry.summary: ROSEBURG — The Roseburg Fire Department will be flushing fire hydrants inside the city limits of Roseburg from April 1 through June 30.

entry.title: BLM closing part of Rice Creek Road starting Friday
entry.summary: WINSTON — The Bureau of Land Management is replacing a deteriorating culvert and will temporarily close a portion of Rice Creek Road, also known as BLM Road 29-7-24.0, starting on Friday.

entry.title: Harvard Medical Park under new ownership
entry.summary: Harvard Medical Park, a campus of medical offices located on West Harvard Avenue in Roseburg, is now under new ownership.
